title: Havasupai Indian Reservation addition : environmental impact statement.

entities.role: governing body of the Havasupai Tribe, plan proponent
entities.quote: The Havasupai Tribal Council designated approximately 6700-plus acres of Pasture Wash as a gardening area.
entities.stance_basis: Tribal Council designated land uses and developed the Land Use Plan for the reservation addition

entities.role: beneficiary tribe and plan author
entities.quote: Now, however, Congress has placed 185,000 acres between Havasu Canyon and Grand Canyon Village in the hands of the Havasupai and they feel the integrity of these lands is assured under their protection.
entities.stance_basis: Tribe authored and supports the Secretarial Land Use Plan for the reservation addition

entities.role: cooperating agency / adjacent land manager
entities.quote: Cooperative enforcement will be worked out between the Tribe and National Park Service for management and enforcement on the Havasupai Traditional Use Area.
entities.stance_basis: Identified as cooperating partner for enforcement; no explicit stance on the plan taken

entities.role: preparing agency / plan administrator
entities.quote: Annual assessments will be made by the Bureau of Indian Affairs Agency Superintendent, pursuant to Section 10(b) and 10(c) of P.L. 93-620 and 25 CFR 151, of the forage, range conditions, and water availability in relation to the livestock and wild game birds to establish and maintain a stable balance and prevent any degradation of the area.
entities.stance_basis: BIA prepared the EIS and is designated as the implementing/oversight agency for the plan

entities.entity: Office of Arid Lands Studies, University of Arizona
entities.role: technical assistance provider
entities.quote: With the Assistance of Office of Arid Lands Studies University of Arizona, Tucson
entities.stance_basis: Listed as technical contributor to the EIS preparation; no independent stance expressed

entities.role: state transportation agency
entities.quote: At this time, an interchange has been constructed on I-40 at the desired location, but there is no road connecting it with Peach Springs, nor are there any plans in the near future by the Arizona State Highway Department to construct one.
entities.stance_basis: Has no plans to construct a related road; no position on the Land Use Plan expressed

entities.role: neighboring tribe / potential joint venture partner
entities.quote: If any road is built, it will likely have to be a joint venture between the Hualapai Indian Tribe and Mohave County, and there are no plans for such a project.
entities.stance_basis: Identified as potential partner for road construction; no explicit stance on the Land Use Plan

entities.role: federal agency with road improvement authority
entities.quote: the amount of traffic to Topocoba Hilltop, potentially the most heavily visited area after Hualapai Hilltop, is heavily dependent on whether or not the U.S. Forest Service improves the road from Tusayan to Pasture Wash.
entities.stance_basis: document notes Forest Service road improvement decision affects tourist traffic outcomes but no position attributed

entities.role: state wildlife agency; research biologist assisted in survey
entities.quote: an Arizona Game and Fish Department research biologist specializing in mountain lion assisted in the survey.
entities.stance_basis: agency biologist participated in survey and offered technical observations on lion population

entities.role: Arizona Game and Fish Department research biologist
entities.quote: The Game Department Biologist surmized that the low deer population prevented a larger number of lions from working the area. His research around Prescott, Arizona also indicates that a cow-calf livestock operation is of considerably more benefit to lions than a yearling steer operation.
entities.stance_basis: biologist offered technical findings on lion population dynamics relative to livestock operations

entities.role: reservation landowner and plan proponent
entities.quote: the tribe alludes to 17 sheep residing in Havasu Canyon.
entities.stance_basis: tribe is the proponent of the land use plan and its proposed developments throughout the document

entities.role: Bureau of Indian Affairs official
entities.quote: Well-managed cow-calf operations of these sizes with 70 to 75% calf crops would gross $37,500 and $45,000 respectively (Bill Beck, B.I.A.).
entities.stance_basis: cited as source for economic projections on cattle operations; no explicit position on the proposal attributed

entities.role: consultant employed by the tribe on waste management
entities.quote: The tribe has employed a reputable consultant to examine the problem in Havasu Creek (Pope, Evans, & Robbins 1973).
entities.stance_basis: cited as consultant engaged to examine water pollution problem; no explicit stance on the proposal

entities.role: federal agency providing soil data
entities.quote: The USDA Soil Conservation Service, General Soil Map of Coconino County lists the development limitations of these series.
entities.stance_basis: cited as data source for soil development limitations; no explicit stance attributed

entities.role: Havasupai tribal governing body
entities.quote: The second provision under Agriculture, the proposed land use code to be developed by the Tribal Council, cannot be evaluated as it presently does not exist.
entities.stance_basis: Tribal Council tasked with developing land use code as part of the plan, indicating support for the proposal

entities.role: tribal governing body, commenter/adopter of Range Management Plan
entities.quote: This letter is to advise that the Havasupai Tribal Council has adopted, Plan A of the Range Management Plan developed by the Kaibab Forest of U,S, National Forest in 1967 This plan is to be used for the Environmental Impact Statement together with the Land Use Plan for lands added to the Havasupai Reservation under PL 93-620,
entities.stance_basis: formally adopted Range Management Plan for use with the EIS

entities.role: Chairman, Havasupai Tribal Council
entities.quote: This letter is to advise that the Havasupai Tribal Council has adopted, Plan A of the Range Management Plan developed by the Kaibab Forest of U,S, National Forest in 1967 This plan is to be used for the Environmental Impact Statement together with the Land Use Plan for lands added to the Havasupai Reservation under PL 93-620,
entities.stance_basis: signed letter confirming tribal adoption of the Range Management Plan for the EIS

entities.role: Havasupai Tribe, project proponent
entities.quote: An alternative, strongly opposed by the tribe, to the proposed continued use of the Hualapai and Topocoba Trails for tourists and the transport of supplies into Havasu Canyon would be the construction of (1) a motor road and (2) a chairlift into Havasu Canyon.
entities.stance_basis: tribe strongly opposed motor road and chairlift alternatives to trail use

entities.role: subject tribe / field assessment consultant
entities.quote: The Tribe will benefit more from the proposed actions than from no action.
entities.stance_basis: narrator attributes greater benefit to the tribe from proposed actions versus no action

entities.role: federal agency, cooperating agency, Truxton Canyon Agency / Phoenix Area Office
entities.quote: an environmental assessment was prepared in December, 1975 by the Truxton Canyon Agency of the Bureau of Indian Affairs in consultation with the Havasupai Tribal Council.
entities.stance_basis: prepared environmental assessment supporting the land use plan process

entities.entity: University of Arizona (Office of Arid Lands Studies)
entities.role: EIS preparer / contractor
entities.quote: The University of Arizona (Office of Arid Lands Studies) was invited during February, 1977 to submit a proposal to prepare an environmental impact statement concerning the 185,000 acre addition.
entities.stance_basis: contracted to prepare the EIS; no independent position on the project attributed

entities.role: federal regulatory body providing EIS guidelines
entities.quote: a Statement Outline was prepared in which specific tasks were outlined using guidelines provided by the Council of Environmental Quality (CEQ).
entities.stance_basis: provided procedural guidelines; no project stance attributed

entities.role: Arizona state official, field assessment consultant
entities.quote: consultation with the State Historic Preservation Officer would be appropriate.
entities.stance_basis: recommended for consultation on cultural resource impacts; no project stance attributed

entities.role: Research Biologist, Arizona Game and Fish Department; field assessment consultant
entities.quote: Aspects of field assessments have been reviewed and consultations provided by the following entities:
entities.stance_basis: listed as providing field assessment consultation; no project stance attributed

entities.role: tribal planning body, co-preparer of Secretarial Land Use Plan
entities.quote: Prepared by: Havasupai Tribe Planning Commit and Phoenix Area Office Bureau of Indian Affairs Department of the Interior
entities.stance_basis: co-prepared the Land Use Plan for the addition to the Havasupai Indian Reservation, expressing tribal desires for the land

entities.entity: Phoenix Area Office, Bureau of Indian Affairs
entities.role: co-preparer of Secretarial Land Use Plan
entities.quote: Prepared by: Havasupai Tribe Planning Commit and Phoenix Area Office Bureau of Indian Affairs Department of the Interior
entities.stance_basis: co-prepared the Land Use Plan implementing the reservation enlargement under P.L. 93-620

entities.role: tribal governing body
entities.quote: The Havasupai Tribal Council interpreted the provisions of P. L. 93-620 as a request for a set of general guidelines and intentions from the Havasupai Tribe.
entities.stance_basis: interpreted and acted upon P.L. 93-620 to develop land use plan expressing tribal goals for the enlarged reservation

entities.role: sponsors of S. 1296 / P.L. 93-620
entities.quote: To all: Congressional sponsors of the measure wro guarantees into the legislation.
entities.stance_basis: wrote safeguards/guarantees into the legislation to protect against uncontrolled development of Havasupai lands

entities.role: state government
entities.quote: The State of Ari zona that this section represents a school sec not be included within the reservation.
entities.stance_basis: argued that Section 13 of Range 6 West is a school section that cannot be included within the reservation

entities.role: tribal nation
entities.quote: The Havasupai argue the 1 never out of public status.
entities.stance_basis: argued that disputed sections were never out of public status and should be included within the reservation

entities.entity: private landowner south of the reservation
entities.role: private landowner
entities.quote: The private lando south of the reservation argues these sec sent private land that the Congress canno public status.
entities.stance_basis: argued that disputed sections represent private land that Congress cannot restore to public status

entities.role: federal agency, cooperating in plan preparation
entities.quote: The tary made the assistance of the Interior available to them whenever this was deeme or requested by the Havasupai Tribal Coun sponsibility for coordinating all necessa and for final preparation and distributio completed plan was delegated to the Burea Affairs by the Secretarial Order of Septe
entities.stance_basis: delegated responsibility for coordinating and finalizing the land use plan, supporting implementation of P.L. 93-620

entities.role: state wildlife agency
entities.quote: the only hunting the Havasupai were allowed, on the lands included in their reservation today, was on the southern part under public permits from the Arizona Game and Fish who furnished the following deer harvest estimates:
entities.stance_basis: furnished deer harvest estimates and issued public hunting permits on Havasupai lands without an attributed position on the project

entities.role: tribal governing body, reservation land manager
entities.quote: The plan for the use of the 185,000 acre addition to the Havasupai Reservation, developed by the Secretary of the Interior in consultation with the Havasupai Tribal Council, pursuant to Section 10(b)(4) of Public Law 93-620, is hereby respectfully submitted to the Committees on Interior and Insular Affairs of the United States Congress
entities.stance_basis: participated in developing the land use plan submitted to Congress

entities.role: reservation landowner and wildlife manager
entities.quote: the Havasupai Tribe will restrict public hunting on the reservation.
entities.stance_basis: supports wildlife management plan including hunting restrictions and wildlife diversity goals on the reservation

entities.role: cooperating agency for wildlife management
entities.quote: the Havasupai Tribal Council ... operate with the National Park Service in
entities.stance_basis: cooperating with Havasupai Tribe in wildlife/exotic species control on the reservation

entities.role: Secretary of the Interior, plan developer and signatory
entities.quote: The plan for the use of the 185,000 acre addition to the Havasupai Reservation, developed by the Secretary of the Interior in consultation with the Havasupai Tribal Council, pursuant to Section 10(b)(4) of Public Law 93-620, is hereby respectfully submitted to the Committees on Interior and Insular Affairs of the United States Congress
entities.stance_basis: developed and submitted the land use plan to Congress

entities.role: Bureau of Indian Affairs, plan signatory
entities.quote: The plan for the use of the 185,000 acre addition to the Havasupai Reservation, developed by the Secretary of the Interior in consultation with the Havasupai Tribal Council, pursuant to Section 10(b)(4) of Public Law 93-620, is hereby respectfully submitted to the Committees on Interior and Insular Affairs of the United States Congress on this date, Area Director, Phoenix Area Office (Date) Commissioner of Indian Affairs (Date) Secretary of Interior (Date)
entities.stance_basis: co-signatory on submission of the land use plan to Congress

entities.role: Bureau of Indian Affairs, Phoenix Area Office, plan signatory
entities.quote: The plan for the use of the 185,000 acre addition to the Havasupai Reservation, developed by the Secretary of the Interior in consultation with the Havasupai Tribal Council, pursuant to Section 10(b)(4) of Public Law 93-620, is hereby respectfully submitted to the Committees on Interior and Insular Affairs of the United States Congress on this date, Area Director, Phoenix Area Office
entities.stance_basis: co-signatory submitting the land use plan to Congress

entities.entity: U. S. Bureau of Sports Fisheries and Wildlife Management
entities.role: wildlife survey authority authorized by Tribal Council
entities.quote: The Tribal Council will then authorize annual resurveys by the U. S. Bureau of Sports Fisheries and Wildlife Management, by the Arizona State Game & Fish Department, or by trained rangers from their own Tribe.
entities.stance_basis: authorized to conduct annual wildlife resurveys under the reservation management plan

entities.role: wildlife survey authority authorized by Tribal Council
entities.quote: The Tribal Council will then authorize annual resurveys by the U. S. Bureau of Sports Fisheries and Wildlife Management, by the Arizona State Game & Fish Department, or by trained rangers from their own Tribe.
entities.stance_basis: authorized to conduct annual wildlife resurveys under the reservation management plan

entities.entity: Phoenix Area Office, Bureau of Indian Affairs
entities.role: land planning section, plan preparer
entities.quote: Prepared By: The Land Planning Section Phoenix Area Office, Bureau of Indian Affairs
entities.stance_basis: prepared the land use and management maps submitted as part of the plan
